input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Declared governing constraint present but constraint-check shows an energy-balance violation a fit metric hides.

case_context.cou: surrogate's field feeds a wall-heat-flux QoI for a cooling-margin decision
case_context.decision_consequence: cooling margin from the derived flux

answer_key.gold_mechanism: A low temperature MAE does not enforce the declared energy balance; the unreconciled energy imbalance biases the derived heat-flux QoI the COU depends on, so a governing constraint is violated despite the good fit.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the heat flux is quantitatively wrong (a verdict beyond the residual)
treats the low temperature MAE as evidence the energy balance holds

hardness.obvious_signal: validation MAE on temperature is excellent (0.4%) — the surrogate looks accurate
hardness.conflicting_signal: the declared energy-conservation constraint is violated (global energy imbalance ~6%), which a temperature MAE does not penalize and which biases derived heat-flux QoIs

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 model-form adequacy: a data-fit metric does not imply governing-constraint satisfaction; an unreconciled conservation residual is a model-form defeater.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
